learnerValue: Tests the nuclear-versus-loop distinction while preserving that plasmids are optional.
Retrieves the core magnification ratio with useful inversion and multiplication distractors.
Rehearses the ordered cell-cycle sequence and distinguishes mitosis from meiosis.
Secures the net high-to-low movement of diffusion while contrasting osmosis.
Builds the cells-to-organism hierarchy as an ordered retrieval chain.
Links each digestive-enzyme group to its substrate and products.
Fixes the right- and left-ventricle destinations in double circulation.
Contrasts xylem and phloem by cargo and the directions stated in the specification.
Combines the pathogen definition, four pathogen groups and three transmission routes.
Retrieves all three specified white-blood-cell defence mechanisms.
Connects vaccine exposure to the rapid, specific response upon re-entry.
Separates antibiotics' viral limitation from painkillers' symptom-only role.
Provides concise retrieval of all four specified photosynthesis-rate factors.
Contrasts aerobic and muscle-anaerobic respiration through their word equations.
Links the three exercise responses to anaerobic respiration, lactic acid and fatigue.
Retrieves the complete definition, including optimum function and responses to change.
Practises the full nervous-response pathway in its correct order.
Connects high blood glucose to insulin, cellular uptake and glycogen storage.
Differentiates the Foundation-tier roles of all four menstrual-cycle hormones.
Reinforces the copy–two divisions–four different gametes sequence.
Builds a useful structural relationship among DNA, chromosomes, genes and the genome.
Contrasts both allele-expression rules and both allele-pair terms.

issues: The quoted wording is from 4.6.2.2 Evolution, not the cited 4.6.3.2 Speciation (biology only), so the source locator is inaccurate.
The curriculum component maps the card to the Darwin-and-Wallace speciation topic rather than the evolution topic that directly contains both tested statements.
learnerValue: The prompt usefully links selection of suited phenotypes to the fertile-offspring boundary between species.
Connects the ecosystem definition to the community-wide consequence of interdependence.
Contrasts the specified roles of the carbon and water cycles in one retrieval route.
Links the definition of biodiversity to its stated contribution to ecosystem stability.
Combines the approximate transfer percentage with the main routes of biomass loss.
